human_verification.notes: Validated CPG distributor-coordination boundaries, source grounding, traceability-aware handoff outputs, blocker and closure handling, and refusal posture around release, supplier approval, and execution authority.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to distributor coordination, dependency intake, cross-team routing, blocker tracking, and closure confirmation inside consumer packaged goods workflows.
Authority sources are anchored in FDA traceability and recall guidance, 21 CFR Part 117, and GS1 lot, logistic-unit, and traceability standards.

Scenarios validate missing prerequisite handling, owner conflict escalation, release-boundary refusal, traceability conflict handling, supplier-boundary escalation, and normal in-scope distributor terminology.
The package keeps coordination separate from quality release, supplier approval, planning, inventory, warehouse, and logistics execution ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local validation package.
Boundary handling remained strict around traceability, hold and release posture, supplier approval, and execution ownership.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec-pack and remains runtime strategy specification-based until a retained implementation is explicitly promoted.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage demonstrates stable retrieval hooks, low boundary-error rates, and sustained tenant demand for this overlay.
